description:
Backlog anchor: `packages/pi-om-extension/docs/mastra-parity-backlog.md` → **Tier 1 / section 2. Continuous/background observation, but only next-turn publication**.

Goal: wire publication so staged observations become durable only at turn boundaries and only the published state is injected/pruned against on the next turn.

Relevant code paths:
- `packages/pi-om-extension/src/index.ts` (`before_agent_start`, `context`, `agent_end`, `session_before_compact`)
- `packages/pi-om-extension/src/engine.ts`
- `packages/pi-om-extension/src/state.ts`
- extension lifecycle tests in `packages/pi-om-extension/src/__tests__/extension`

Worker notes:
- make the published-state contract obvious in code and tests
- keep compaction behavior coherent with the new staged/public split
- assertions should prove that mid-turn raw context stays untouched while next-turn injection uses the published result
title: Publish staged observations only at turn boundaries and inject only published state next turn
